free_text: Fluctuations in the integrated cross section of the
reaction 45Sc(p,n)45Ti
Measured by 5 keV step
A thin target of chemically pure scandium metal
evaporated in vacuum onto thick aluminium backing
An excitation function of the gamma rays from
27Al(p,g)28Si was first obtained in the region of the
resonances at 1.38 MeV by bombarding a thick
aluminium blank with protons by varying the energy in
steps of 2.5 keV.
Then an excitation function of the gamma rays from the
same reaction was again obtained in a similar
manner in the same energy region by bombarding the Sc
target onto thick aluminium backing.
Derived thermonuclear
reaction rates given
Compiled by B.Jyrwa, Physics Department,
North Eastern Hill University, Shillong & Prajesh
Prajapati, RPDD, BARC, Mumbai India
Re-compiled by Reetuparna Ghosh, B.Jyrwa,
Physics  Department, NEHU, Shillong, Meghalaya, and
Paresh Prajapati, RDPP, BARC, Mumbai India
On. BIB updated (formal correction)
VS. REL-REF added.
The absolute errors on the measured cross
section is 25%. The errors due to counting statistics
are smaller than the size of the points.
Fig.1 of Nucl.Phys.A 96(1967)521
On. Re-digitized
